titleStmt.title: Letter from Fellow & Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland to the Precentor, St. Patrick's Cathedral, 9 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Fellow and Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) to the precentor of St. Patrick's Cathedral in Dublin requesting twelve tickets for the Kitchener Memorial Service taking place in the cathedral the following Tuesday.
In 1916 Reverend Hugh Jackson Lawlor (1860-1938) was the acting precentor of St. Patrick's Cathedral Dublin. Lawlor held the position from 1902 until 1924. Aside from his role as a clergyman Lawlor was well regarded for his learning and an active member of the Royal Irish Academy.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.persName: Fellow and Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
9th JUNE 16. The Revd.The Precentor,
St.Patrick's Cathedral,
Dublin. Dear Sir, The President and Fellows of the Royal College of
Physicians of Ireland desire to attend the Kitchener
Memorial Service in your Cathedral on Tuesday next the 13th
instant and I shall feel greatly obliged if you will kindly
let me have 12 admission tickets and reserve a pew for the
College representatives. I remain,
Yours faithfully, Fellow & Registrar.
Royal College of Physicians of Ireland.
Kildare Street,
Dublin. 3rd JUNE 1916. A. Millere
